title: Queen For A Day

jokes.text: One letter titled 'If You Weren't My Cousin,' was particularly incriminating.
jokes.setup: George Michael writing love letters to his cousin Maeby
jokes.punchline: The specific title revealing the uncomfortable romantic nature
jokes.explanation: Perfect character-driven cringe comedy. The title is hilariously specific and inappropriate, perfectly capturing George Michael's awkward romantic feelings.

jokes.text: Plus, a lot of kids are still talking about when you took down that homecoming banner.
jokes.setup: George Michael embarrassed by his father's stair car
jokes.punchline: Reference to previous incident involving the impractical vehicle
jokes.explanation: Good callback establishing the ongoing embarrassment of the stair car and its impracticality.

jokes.text: It's not a car. But- But, no.
jokes.setup: Michael asking if George Michael is embarrassed by the 'car'
jokes.punchline: George Michael's technical correction followed by quick denial
jokes.explanation: Great economy and character work. George Michael's polite but honest correction reveals his embarrassment while trying not to hurt his father's feelings.

jokes.text: Marry me. - Ha! I need your notes on those tomorrow.
jokes.setup: Movie executive hitting on young-looking Maeby
jokes.punchline: Maeby completely deflecting romantic advance with work talk
jokes.explanation: Sharp misdirection showing Maeby's professional focus and maturity despite her actual age. Good economy in the exchange.

jokes.text: I think the age difference is really starting to catch up to us.
jokes.setup: Buster talking about his relationship with his mother
jokes.punchline: Treating their mother-son relationship like a romantic one with age gap concerns
jokes.explanation: Brilliant character comedy. Buster's complete obliviousness to how inappropriate his codependent relationship with his mother sounds when described in romantic terms.

jokes.text: Meetin' women. Meetin' women who didn't give birth to you.
jokes.setup: Michael suggesting Buster meet people
jokes.punchline: Having to specify the obvious boundary that shouldn't need stating
jokes.explanation: Perfect escalation and character moment. Michael having to specify this obvious boundary highlights how dysfunctional the family dynamics are.

jokes.text: My schedule, however, is as open as my relationship with my wife.
jokes.setup: Tobias responding to Michael's busy schedule
jokes.punchline: Comparing his availability to his failing marriage using parallel structure
jokes.explanation: Excellent wordplay with perfect economy. Tobias inadvertently reveals his marital problems through a clever parallel construction.

jokes.text: Even if it means me taking a chubby, I will suck it up.
jokes.setup: Tobias offering to be Michael's wingman
jokes.punchline: Innocent use of phrase with unintended sexual connotation
jokes.explanation: Classic Tobias double entendre. His complete obliviousness to the sexual implications of his phrasing is perfectly in character.

jokes.text: Boy, that's enough family stuff for today.
jokes.setup: Tobias's inappropriate sexual innuendo
jokes.punchline: Michael's understated reaction to the awkwardness
jokes.explanation: Good reaction beat. Michael's deadpan response perfectly captures his discomfort with his family's inappropriate behavior.

jokes.text: I call it Single City... I call it Swing City
jokes.setup: Gob presenting business ideas with naming convention
jokes.punchline: Lazy reuse of the same naming pattern for different concepts
jokes.explanation: Good character comedy showing Gob's lack of creativity and business acumen through his repetitive naming convention.

jokes.text: But check your lease, man, because you're livin' in [pause] City.
jokes.setup: Gob continuing his 'City' naming pattern
jokes.punchline: Pause where he realizes he can't think of an appropriate word
jokes.explanation: Brilliant character moment. The awkward pause where Gob realizes he's painted himself into a corner with his naming convention is perfectly executed cringe comedy.

jokes.text: I took his dress eyebrows.
jokes.setup: Gob explaining his revenge on Sitwell
jokes.punchline: Stealing eyebrows from a hairless man, calling them 'dress eyebrows'
jokes.explanation: Wonderfully absurd and original. The phrase 'dress eyebrows' is both specific and ridiculous, perfectly capturing Gob's petty vindictiveness.

jokes.text: especially when he goes to the opera with two mustaches on his forehead.
jokes.setup: Sitwell wearing Gob's stolen 'dress eyebrows'
jokes.punchline: Visual image of formal eyebrows looking like forehead mustaches
jokes.explanation: Great visual comedy. The specific image of formal eyebrows looking like mustaches at the opera is both ridiculous and vivid.

jokes.text: I thought you won that on Card Sharks.
jokes.setup: Learning about Gob's Camaro
jokes.punchline: Reference to family mythology about how Gob acquired the car
jokes.explanation: Good callback that reveals the family's pattern of creating false narratives to protect Michael's feelings.

jokes.text: Michael, I was never on Card Sharks. Dad told us not to tell you.
jokes.setup: Michael's belief about the Card Sharks story
jokes.punchline: Revelation that it was a coordinated family lie
jokes.explanation: Excellent revelation that recontextualizes the family dynamic. Shows how they protect Michael while also revealing their dishonesty.

jokes.text: At least in prison, we had knife fights, and we had movie night. And once both. Those men did not enjoy Soapdish.
jokes.setup: George Sr. comparing prison favorably to the attic
jokes.punchline: Combining knife fights with movie night, revealing the specific movie
jokes.explanation: Brilliant escalation and specificity. The combination of violence and entertainment, capped by the specific reference to Soapdish, is perfectly absurd and memorable.

jokes.text: I think you have to know that world.
jokes.setup: Prisoners not enjoying Soapdish during a knife fight
jokes.punchline: George Sr.'s analytical explanation for their reaction
jokes.explanation: Perfect deadpan delivery. George Sr.'s matter-of-fact film criticism in the context of prison violence is beautifully absurd.

jokes.text: I'm driving a staircase, for God's sake. It smells like gas. I get hop-ons.
jokes.setup: Michael complaining about his transportation
jokes.punchline: List of increasingly specific problems with the stair car
jokes.explanation: Great escalation of the stair car's problems. 'Hop-ons' is a perfectly specific and absurd term for people jumping onto a moving staircase.

jokes.text: Well, of course you're gonna get hop-ons.
jokes.setup: Michael complaining about hop-ons
jokes.punchline: George Sr. treating this absurd problem as completely logical
jokes.explanation: Perfect deadpan response. George Sr.'s matter-of-fact acceptance of the stair car's inherent problems highlights its absurdity.

jokes.text: I gave you company, which, apparently, you don't want to give to me.
jokes.setup: Michael complaining about not getting a company car
jokes.punchline: George Sr.'s play on 'company' (business vs. companionship)
jokes.explanation: Clever wordplay that reveals character. George Sr.'s manipulation through guilt while also showing his loneliness is well-crafted.

jokes.text: Pop-Pop gets a Grisham?
jokes.setup: George Sr. asking for company and books
jokes.punchline: Referring to himself in third person as 'Pop-Pop' while requesting specific author
jokes.explanation: Brilliantly pathetic character moment. The third-person reference combined with the specific author request creates perfect absurd pathos.

jokes.text: Moms are such a pain in the ass, huh? It's like 'Die already!'
jokes.setup: Starla trying to bond with Buster about mothers
jokes.punchline: Extremely inappropriate suggestion about mothers dying
jokes.explanation: Good cringe comedy. Starla's misreading of Buster's relationship with his mother creates uncomfortable dark humor.

jokes.text: I love my mother. She's one of the two most important people in my life. You know, she and Quincy.
jokes.setup: Starla's inappropriate comment about mothers
jokes.punchline: Buster's earnest response including mysterious reference to Quincy
jokes.explanation: Perfect character moment showing Buster's innocent devotion and raising questions about Quincy. Good setup for the reveal that follows.

jokes.text: A spokesman for Mr. Jones denies they've ever met... although he could not explain the six signed gold records in Starla's apartment.
jokes.setup: Buster mentioning Quincy as important person
jokes.punchline: Reveal that Starla is delusionally connected to Quincy Jones
jokes.explanation: Excellent misdirection. The reveal recontextualizes Buster's comment and establishes Starla's delusions in a specific, absurd way.

jokes.text: I think I proved that with [blank] Mountain.
jokes.setup: Gob claiming to be an 'ideas man'
jokes.punchline: Reference to incomplete or failed project with missing word
jokes.explanation: Good callback showing Gob's pattern of incomplete ideas. The missing word suggests either censorship or his inability to name his own project.

jokes.text: Did you want me to stay and do that?
jokes.setup: Gob claiming determination to prove himself
jokes.punchline: Immediately asking if he needs to follow through
jokes.explanation: Perfect character moment. Gob's immediate backtrack from his passionate declaration reveals his complete lack of actual determination.

jokes.text: Maybe a puck hit me. It could happen.
jokes.setup: Barry explaining his lawsuit against the LA Kings
jokes.punchline: Uncertain about whether the injury actually occurred
jokes.explanation: Good character moment for Barry showing his questionable legal practices and general incompetence.

jokes.text: I'll tell you one thing you don't want to do is buy stock in the L.A. Kings.
jokes.setup: Michael asking about stock trading
jokes.punchline: Barry giving irrelevant investment advice about the team he's suing
jokes.explanation: Good misdirection showing Barry's tendency to give irrelevant advice and his single-minded focus on his lawsuit.

jokes.text: Why do you want me to write it?
jokes.setup: Michael asking Barry to draft a letter
jokes.punchline: Barry questioning why he should do the legal work he's paid for
jokes.explanation: Good character comedy showing Barry's lazy approach to his profession and general incompetence.

jokes.text: That's a bad title.
jokes.setup: George Sr. finding and reading 'Armageddon' script
jokes.punchline: Matter-of-fact criticism of major Hollywood blockbuster
jokes.explanation: Perfect economy and character moment. George Sr.'s blunt criticism shows his confidence in his opinions despite his circumstances.

jokes.text: We have, like, 400 of these... Oh, they're not popular. We have 400 of them.
jokes.setup: Michael thinking the car is popular
jokes.punchline: Clarification that they have so many because no one wants them
jokes.explanation: Good misdirection with nice deadpan delivery. The salesman's honesty about the car's unpopularity is refreshingly blunt.

jokes.text: Oh, you're the stair car guy. You took down our banner.
jokes.setup: Michael mentioning the stair car
jokes.punchline: Recognition of Michael's embarrassing public incident
jokes.explanation: Good callback showing how Michael's embarrassing moments follow him around town.

jokes.text: I got a hop-on.
jokes.setup: Explaining the banner incident
jokes.punchline: Using the absurd term 'hop-on' as a valid excuse
jokes.explanation: Good callback and character moment. Michael treating 'hop-ons' as a legitimate excuse shows how normalized the stair car's problems have become.

jokes.text: They throw these extras at you because it's so impractical. No back seat. It attracts so many tickets... the insurance alone costs as much as the last three cars we saw.
jokes.setup: Salesman explaining the sports car's features
jokes.punchline: List of increasingly expensive problems with the impractical car
jokes.explanation: Good escalation showing how the car salesman's honesty reveals increasingly bad aspects of the 'deal.'

jokes.text: I'll take it.
jokes.setup: Long list of the car's expensive problems
jokes.punchline: Michael's immediate decision to buy it anyway
jokes.explanation: Great character moment showing Michael's impulsive decision-making and desire to finally have something nice, despite the obvious problems.

jokes.text: Lucille 2! - Buster! - So you're back. - Oh, I'm back, Buster!
jokes.setup: Buster and Lucille 2 meeting at the club
jokes.punchline: Their over-enthusiastic reunion immediately triggering her vertigo
jokes.explanation: Good physical comedy setup showing the immediate incompatibility between Buster's enthusiasm and Lucille 2's medical condition.

jokes.text: And I am rock steady! No more dizzies.
jokes.setup: Lucille 2 claiming to be cured of vertigo
jokes.punchline: Immediate contradiction as she becomes dizzy from the reunion
jokes.explanation: Classic dramatic irony. Her confident declaration immediately before losing her balance creates perfect comedic timing.

jokes.text: Well, I would hardly call my mother a girl.
jokes.setup: Lucille 2 asking if there's a girl in his life
jokes.punchline: Buster's literal interpretation focusing on his mother
jokes.explanation: Perfect character moment showing Buster's innocent literal-mindedness and his mother-centric worldview.

jokes.text: Oh. Well, she mostly likes talk radio.
jokes.setup: Lucille 2 asking about someone who makes him hear music
jokes.punchline: Buster's literal response about his mother's radio preferences
jokes.explanation: Excellent misdirection and character work. Buster's completely missing the romantic implication and focusing on his mother is perfectly in character.

jokes.text: Everybody thinks they're Frank Sinatra.
jokes.setup: Man singing loudly and badly at the club
jokes.punchline: Lucille 2's diplomatic but cutting observation
jokes.explanation: Good observational humor that establishes the club's atmosphere and Lucille 2's personality.

jokes.text: That was half my fault. I thought I saw a graham cracker out there.
jokes.setup: Buster being locked on the balcony
jokes.punchline: His innocent explanation for going outside
jokes.explanation: Perfect character moment. Buster's innocent explanation for falling into his mother's trap reveals his childlike nature and sweet tooth.

jokes.text: You baited the balcony? - Prove it.
jokes.setup: Revelation that Lucille deliberately lured Buster outside
jokes.punchline: Lucille's unapologetic challenge when caught
jokes.explanation: Excellent character moment showing Lucille's manipulative nature and complete lack of shame when confronted.

jokes.text: By the way, I love your mother. - Oh, yeah, I do too. - Oh.
jokes.setup: Starla making conversation with Michael
jokes.punchline: Michael's casual response misunderstood as inappropriate agreement
jokes.explanation: Good misdirection creating awkward moment. The misunderstanding about which mother they're discussing creates uncomfortable comedy.

jokes.text: Wait a minute. This tub's a little bigger than it used to be.
jokes.setup: Michael noticing something different about the bathroom
jokes.punchline: Realization that home renovation has been done without permission
jokes.explanation: Good setup for the reveal of Lucille's property theft. Michael's understated observation sets up the bigger revelation.

jokes.text: I moved the wall a couple of inches into her penthouse.
jokes.setup: Michael noticing the bathroom changes
jokes.punchline: Lucille's casual admission of property theft
jokes.explanation: Excellent escalation and character moment. Lucille's matter-of-fact confession of stealing property reveals her complete disregard for boundaries.

jokes.text: It does? I stopped at 'unfrozen.'
jokes.setup: Michael explaining the letter warned against selling stock
jokes.punchline: Lucille's admission she didn't read the important part
jokes.explanation: Perfect character moment showing Lucille's selective attention and impulsive decision-making. Great economy in the delivery.

jokes.text: Oh, he really, really did look like a woman.
jokes.setup: Tobias explaining his accidental business purchase
jokes.punchline: His delayed realization about the club owner's appearance
jokes.explanation: Good character moment showing Tobias's confusion about gender and his tendency toward awkward observations.

jokes.text: But anyhoo, can you believe that the only reason the club is going under... is because it's in a terrifying neighborhood?
jokes.setup: Tobias explains why he could buy the Queen Mary club
jokes.punchline: His oblivious assessment of the neighborhood danger
jokes.explanation: Good character obliviousness. Tobias's casual dismissal of genuine danger shows his sheltered perspective.

jokes.text: Hey, Dad, is that your new car in the driveway? I can't believe you got a Corvette!
jokes.setup: Michael buying an expensive sports car while criticizing family spending
jokes.punchline: George Michael's excited recognition of his father's hypocrisy
jokes.explanation: Good ironic moment highlighting Michael's hypocrisy. George Michael's innocent excitement makes the contradiction more pointed.

jokes.text: You can't see it. I just winked.
jokes.setup: Barry has an eye patch
jokes.punchline: His inability to properly wink with the patch
jokes.explanation: Good physical comedy. Barry's acknowledgment of his failed wink due to the eye patch is perfectly delivered.

jokes.text: - That's what they said on AskJeeves. - All right, who's the majority shareholder now? - A company called Standpoor.
jokes.setup: Barry explains his research source
jokes.punchline: His reliance on AskJeeves for legal advice
jokes.explanation: Good character moment. Barry using AskJeeves for serious legal research shows his incompetence perfectly.

jokes.text: Standpoor? - As in the opposite of Sitwell...
jokes.setup: Mysterious company name 'Standpoor'
jokes.punchline: Explanation of the wordplay revealing Lucille 2's involvement
jokes.explanation: Excellent wordplay that's both clever and character-specific. The pun reveals plot information while being genuinely witty.

jokes.text: - You're wearing his eyebrows? - They make me feel dressier.
jokes.setup: Michael notices Gob is still wearing Sitwell's eyebrows
jokes.punchline: Gob's sincere explanation for keeping them
jokes.explanation: Excellent callback. Gob's sincere belief that stolen eyebrows make him dressier is perfectly absurd.

jokes.text: Standpoor, because she can't stand up without falling over.
jokes.setup: The Standpoor company name mystery
jokes.punchline: Lucille's crude explanation of the disability-based pun
jokes.explanation: Good wordplay that reveals character. Lucille's insensitive explanation shows her cruel nature while explaining the pun.

jokes.text: I don't know why she'd have any trouble. A piano could stand on those legs.
jokes.setup: Discussion of Lucille 2's balance problems
jokes.punchline: Lucille's backhanded compliment about her legs
jokes.explanation: Good character moment showing Lucille's ability to insult while seeming to compliment. The piano comparison is both specific and cutting.

jokes.text: Sweet ride, dude. - Let's give her Buster.
jokes.setup: Starla innocently admiring Michael's car
jokes.punchline: Lucille immediately returning to her plan to pimp out her son
jokes.explanation: Excellent juxtaposition showing Lucille's single-minded focus on her scheme. The contrast between Starla's innocent comment and Lucille's response is perfectly dark.

jokes.text: Don't you think you belong in a Lucille?
jokes.setup: Michael giving Buster advice about cars/women
jokes.punchline: Using car model name to suggest romantic partner
jokes.explanation: Clever wordplay that works on multiple levels. The car/woman metaphor is well-executed and character-appropriate for Michael's manipulative moment.

jokes.text: I guess she didn't see the drawings.
jokes.setup: Maeby praising George Michael's script notes
jokes.punchline: Relief that she missed the inappropriate romantic illustrations
jokes.explanation: Perfect callback and cringe moment. George Michael's relief reveals the inappropriate nature of his artwork while maintaining the cousin romance tension.

jokes.text: No, that is not what it says. I distinct- Hello?
jokes.setup: Tobias trying to correct the sign maker
jokes.punchline: Being hung up on mid-word during his complaint
jokes.explanation: Good character moment showing Tobias's powerlessness and tendency toward verbose complaints that people ignore.

jokes.text: Well, I suppose I could just paint over 'Mary.'
jokes.setup: Tobias can't get the sign corrected
jokes.punchline: His solution to just remove 'Mary' from the name
jokes.explanation: Great escalation. Tobias's solution would create 'Tobias's Queen' which has obvious inappropriate implications he doesn't see.

jokes.text: So maybe you could, uh, start jeté-ing... and stop 'je-terrorizing' me.
jokes.setup: Tobias trying to reform the tough guys with theater
jokes.punchline: Ballet/terrorism pun in his earnest appeal
jokes.explanation: Brilliant wordplay that perfectly captures Tobias's character. The ballet/terrorism pun is both clever and perfectly suited to his theatrical pretensions.

jokes.text: You didn't sell your stock? What? No. Your letter said not to... and I would never want to hurt the family.
jokes.setup: Michael asks if Buster sold stock
jokes.punchline: Buster's loyalty despite family dysfunction
jokes.explanation: Good character contrast. Buster's loyalty to family despite their treatment of him shows his genuine nature.

jokes.text: Let her down easy. That right knee could go at any time.
jokes.setup: Michael offering to handle the Lucille 2 breakup
jokes.punchline: Physical warning disguised as emotional advice
jokes.explanation: Excellent wordplay combining emotional advice with physical warning. Michael's consideration for Lucille 2's medical condition adds unexpected depth.

jokes.text: Wow. You really have made some amazing strides in a half an hour.
jokes.setup: Tobias sees the fake criminals performing
jokes.punchline: His amazement at their rapid theatrical improvement
jokes.explanation: Good character obliviousness. Tobias's amazement at professional strippers' performance skills is perfectly naive.

jokes.text: So you think you're tough / Well, we hate rappin' / But don't bust a cap in / 'Cause here's what's happenin' / We're breakin' out some old-fashioned tappin'
jokes.setup: Gang confrontation in dangerous neighborhood
jokes.punchline: Breaking into inappropriate musical theater number
jokes.explanation: Brilliantly absurd sequence. The contrast between dangerous street confrontation and innocent musical theater is perfectly executed and completely in character for Tobias.

jokes.text: Oh, I've been shot! - We miscalculated! Retreat! - Quick, switch to Follies!
jokes.setup: Musical theater number meeting real violence
jokes.punchline: Tobias's response to gunfire is to change musical numbers
jokes.explanation: Perfect escalation and character moment. Tobias's suggestion to switch to Follies in response to gunfire is peak absurdist character comedy.

jokes.text: I have always had a standing order to buy up any available Bluth stock.
jokes.setup: Michael assuming Lucille 2 bought stock out of spite
jokes.punchline: Revelation that she's been supporting the family all along
jokes.explanation: Good misdirection that reframes the entire conflict. Lucille 2's loyalty to the family despite their treatment of her adds emotional depth.

jokes.text: My mom really took up a bunch of your space, huh? What a bitch.
jokes.setup: Gob talks to Lucille 2 about the wall situation
jokes.punchline: His casual insult of his own mother
jokes.explanation: Perfect Gob character moment. His casual betrayal of his mother to impress someone is perfectly selfish.

jokes.text: My mom really took up a bunch of your space, huh? What a bitch.
jokes.setup: Gob trying to bond with Lucille 2
jokes.punchline: Casually insulting his own mother to curry favor
jokes.explanation: Perfect character moment showing Gob's complete lack of loyalty and his willingness to betray family for personal gain.

jokes.text: Oh, I meant my problems. Yeah, she's gonna make me the new president.
jokes.setup: Gob claiming he fixed everything
jokes.punchline: Clarification that he only fixed his own situation
jokes.explanation: Good misdirection revealing Gob's selfishness. His clarification that he only cared about his own problems is perfectly in character.

jokes.text: I've wanted a small family restaurant since I got the Kings settlement. It still has the dungeon area, right?
jokes.setup: Barry taking over the Queen Mary
jokes.punchline: His specific interest in the dungeon area for his restaurant
jokes.explanation: Good dark comedy showing Barry's questionable judgment. His specific interest in keeping the dungeon for a family restaurant is perfectly unsettling.

tmdb_overview: Michael sells a portion of his stock in the Bluth company to buy a car, and Lucille 2 returns, only to discover that Buster is dating the company secretary Starla.
insight: Seventy-one jokes in 27 minutes—absurdist character work hits peak density for Season 2.
